method_atom: A framework for constructing trace-like and residue functionals on a filtered, bigraded associative algebra: one attaches to each element a parameterized spectral function of the form Tr(A P^{-s}), proves it has a meromorphic continuation to the whole complex plane (allowing higher-order poles), and reads off invariant functionals from the pole structure. The two-index grading (by order and by highest log-power) lets one build a hierarchy of "higher" residue functionals on graded subspaces, while an algebraic obstruction argument shows the full algebra admits no nontrivial trace, and a separate finite-part construction yields a canonical linear extension of the ordinary trace.
object_atom: An algebra of pseudodifferential operators whose symbols are log-polyhomogeneous — expansions in homogeneous terms multiplied by integer powers of log|ξ| — together with the noncommutative (Wodzicki) residue, the generalized zeta-function of an elliptic operator, and the Kontsevich-Vishik trace defined on this class.
brief_reason: The graded-algebra, meromorphic zeta-continuation and trace-obstruction machinery is transferable to any filtered operator/algebraic system; the log-polyhomogeneous pseudodifferential operators and their noncommutative-residue and Kontsevich-Vishik functionals are the analysis-specific object.
